hypothesis.statement: Conditional on a scheduled FOMC statement/press-conference window, intraday forward behavior over short horizons may differ systematically from non-event sessions; we test whether the pre-event-knowable proximity-to-scheduled-FOMC conditioner relates to forward outcomes. This is a falsifiable conditioning hypothesis, not a profitability claim.
hypothesis.economic_rationale: Scheduled monetary-policy information arrival concentrates positioning and hedging flow into a known window; behavior in that window is plausibly governed by a different process than ambient price action. Diagnostics decide whether any relationship exists.

new.name: minutes_to_scheduled_event
new.reason: No scheduled-macro-event proximity feature exists; would extend SESSION_CALENDAR_ROLL via a new FeatureRequest (freq_) with a known-ahead event-schedule metadata surface analogous to SessionCalendarRollMetadata.expiration_ts_by_contract_id, carrying point-in-time provenance (metadata.available_ts <= row.available_ts).

new.name: in_fomc_event_window_flag
new.reason: Binary known-ahead window membership flag; same new FeatureRequest family extension.

lookahead_risk.description: Two distinct hazards: (1) the scheduled FOMC TIME must be carried as known-ahead metadata, not back-filled from realized volatility; (2) any FOMC SURPRISE magnitude (decision vs consensus, dot-plot deltas) is only knowable AFTER the release and must never become a live feature.
lookahead_risk.mitigation: Scheduled times use the existing point-in-time metadata pattern with the _require_metadata_known_as_of guard (metadata.available_ts <= row.available_ts). Surprise magnitude, if used at all, is an OFFLINE diagnostic conditioner / labels-only split and is excluded from any live feature contract.

expected_orthogonality_to_dead_mechanism.statement: The conditioner is a known-ahead calendar position, not a price/quote-derived state; it is structurally distinct from the rejected price-action (regime, vwap_session) and BBO (bbo_tradability, liquidity_pa) main effects, which condition on realized price/quote dynamics. Expected-orthogonal by construction; the diagnostic, not this statement, decides.
expected_orthogonality_to_dead_mechanism.diagnostic_check: Residualize forward outcomes against the dead-mechanism factors (regime/vwap_session/bbo_tradability/liquidity_pa) before scoring the event conditioner, and report the conditioner's marginal relationship on the residual.

capacity_turnover_note: Approximately 8 scheduled FOMC windows per year; very low occurrence count drives an N_eff-limited test. Per the Compass Track-B pooled minimum, pool across instruments (ES/NQ/RTY and related) rather than testing one instrument. No capacity or turnover dollar claim is made.

data_dependency.detail: FOMC scheduled meeting dates/times are publicly available and could be encoded as a small known-ahead calendar; FOMC SURPRISE magnitudes require an external economic-data feed. No event-calendar data source exists in the repo today (zero hits for fomc/cpi/nfp). Sourcing any external macro feed is deferred.

value_free_status.claims: hypothesis pre-registration only; no alpha/tradability/profitability claim
